Y as consonant at start of word
SH at the end of a word
AI used in middle of word (Rule 9)
AY at end of base word (Rule 9)
EE vowel team — reliable long E
OA vowel team says long O
OA — first vowel says its name
OO says short /oo/ as in book
AY at end of base word

Y as long I in one syllable

AR — R controls the vowel sound
IR — sounds same as ER and UR
GN consonant team — G is silent

Silent W in WR + silent E for long I
Silent K in KN + silent E for long I

E guards V; UR is R-controlled
Silent E guards V at end

E softens G to /j/ and makes A long
DGE after short E for /j/ sound

LL after short E in one-syllable word

-FUL suffix; keep silent E before consonant suffix

OY at end of base; ED says /d/
UE — E guards U at end of word

GN team — G is silent before N

I before E; E guards V
